peer_exemplars.reasoning: Openness to diverse information sources and fairness concerns drive mild support, but her very low extraversion and digital deprivation limit enthusiasm.
Libertarian preference for choice and anti-domination (66%) support user control over algorithms, but low digital literacy and no device access reduce personal relevance and intensity.

High openness and GAL-TAN participatory democracy preference support user choice, but very low conscientiousness and low digital confidence yield only mild, lukewarm support.
Libertarian preference for choice supports mandates, but low digital literacy and low conscientiousness lead to ambivalence about implementation.

Anti-domination values and high openness support user choice, but low digital literacy and intermittent access limit the perceived importance.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Despite high institutional trust, very low agreeableness and strong populist anti-elitism produce a mildly negative personal stance toward mainstream journalism.
Extremely low trust in all institutions (mean ~4%) and high ethnic nationalism lead to strong distrust of mainstream journalism as an elite, out-group-aligned source.
Very high trust in institutions and experts, combined with low openness and high loyalty, drive strong trust in mainstream journalism.

High trust in institutions and very high agreeableness support moderate trust, but low news-seeking behavior and high collective narcissism about ingroup narratives temper confidence in mainstream outlets.
Very high trust in institutions and experts, combined with high agreeableness and low authoritarianism, strongly favor trust in mainstream journalism.

Extremely low trust in institutions (23.25), high anti-elitism (74.42), and a Manichean worldview (90.41) produce strong distrust of mainstream journalism, consistent with the profile's populist and nationalist orientation.
High trust in institutions (police, judiciary, government) and system justification produce clear trust in mainstream journalism, despite low openness and moderate cultural conservatism.
Low trust in institutions and a populist worldview, combined with moderate political trust scores, produce mild distrust of mainstream journalism.
